pattern: Because x <= y and x + y = n, x alone can never exceed n/2 -- so a single sieve of primality up to n paired with a half-range scan finds every pair already in sorted order.
hints: For any pair (x, y) with x <= y and x + y = n, x can never be more than n/2 -- so there is no need to look anywhere past the halfway point.
Precompute which numbers up to n are prime with a sieve rather than testing each candidate's primality from scratch, since the same range of numbers gets checked over and over.

Scan x from 2 up to n/2 in increasing order; whenever both x and n - x are marked prime, record the pair -- scanning in this order already produces the pairs sorted by increasing x, with no extra sort needed.

edge_inputs.description: two prime pairs including a repeated value pair
n itself equal to twice a prime
n where the only pair is (2, n-2)
several prime pairs across a mid-sized even n
n is odd, so any pair must include 2 as the only even prime
n odd with no valid pair at all

stress.description: n at the maximum constraint, sieve over a million values
